model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Lehigh holds a slight edge in advanced metrics, ranking 284th in KenPom compared to Prairie View A&M's 291st, with a marginally better adjusted offensive efficiency that should exploit the Panthers' weaker defense. While Prairie View A&M has a balanced record, Lehigh's recent Patriot League championship demonstrates clutch performance and tournament readiness. The underdog's path relies on forcing turnovers and winning the rebounding battle, but Lehigh's proven form and superior efficiency metrics make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Team Quality and Matchup Specifics
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated both teams' advanced metrics, recent form, and potential injury concerns to determine the most likely winner.
reasoningStep.evidence: Lehigh ranks 284th in KenPom (adj_em -10.38) vs Prairie View A&M's 291st (adj_em -11.1); Lehigh's adjusted offensive efficiency (102.8) is slightly better than Prairie View's (101.1); Lehigh recently won the Patriot League championship, indicating strong recent form; no major injury reports found for either team.

result.title: Lehigh Receives 16-Seed, Will Face Prairie View A&M In First Four
result.snippet: BETHLEHEM, Pa. - After winning their fourth Patriot League championship on Wednesday, Lehigh men's basketball has received its seeding and opponent for the 2026 NCAA Tournament. The 16th-seeded Mountain Hawks will face the 16th-seeded Prairie View A&M Panthers in the First Four in Dayton, Ohio on Wednesday, March 18 at 6:40 p.m.

result.title: Lehigh vs Prairie View A&M Prediction, Starting Lineups and Injury ...
result.snippet: For the second day of the First Four round of March Madness , the Prairie View A&M Panthers take on the Lehigh Mountain Hawks for a spot in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. Both teams barely made it to the big dance, and they have a lot to prove coming into this contest. The winner moves on to take on No. 1 Florida in the first round.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
